titleStmt.title: Letter from Charles Munro to North Circular Laundry Co., 31 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Charles Arthur Munro

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Charles Munro to North Circular Laundry regarding the damaged wall between Mountjoy Prison and the North Circular Laundry Company.
Charles Arthur Munro (1869-1952) was the governor of Mountjoy prison in Dublin. He is also the brother of the noted author H.H. Munro, better known through his literary pseudonym, Saki.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
From the GOVERNOR,
H. M. PRISON, Mountjoy, 31st May 1916. To
Messrs The North Circular Laundry Co North Circular Road,
D U B L I N. With reference to you letter of the 10th ultimo, I am to enquire
whether this matter could not be satisfactorily met so far as your
Company is concerned by the Company erecting a substantial wooden fence,
subject to a contribution of one half the cost by the Prison Board. (Sd) C.A.Munro,
Governor.
